summary: Backend testing for trade style renaming feature. All 22 tests passed. Successfully verified new style names (scalp, intraday, multi_day, swing, position), correct weight values, backwards compatibility aliases (move_2_move, trade_2_hold, a_plus), TradeStyle enum updates, LiveAlert defaults, and API endpoint acceptance.

TQS_STYLE_WEIGHTS.details: New names (scalp, intraday, multi_day, swing, position) exist with correct weights

BACKWARDS_COMPATIBILITY.details: Old names (move_2_move, trade_2_hold, a_plus) still work and return same weights as new names

TRADESTYLE_ENUM.details: TradeStyle enum has SCALP, INTRADAY, MULTI_DAY with correct values and backwards compat aliases

STYLE_WEIGHT_EXPLANATION.details: get_style_weight_explanation works for both new and old names

INFER_TRADE_STYLE.details: _infer_trade_style returns new style names (scalp, swing, intraday)

API_ENDPOINTS.details: POST /api/tqs/score accepts both new and old style names, response includes trade_style field

features_verified.new_style_names: PASS - scalp, intraday, multi_day, swing, position all present in STYLE_WEIGHTS
features_verified.api_old_styles: PASS - /api/tqs/score accepts move_2_move, trade_2_hold, a_plus (backwards compat)

critical_code_review_comments: Trade style renaming is complete and correct
Backwards compatibility is properly maintained - old code using move_2_move, trade_2_hold, a_plus will continue to work
A+ now correctly refers to GRADE (quality) not STYLE - good clarification
TradeStyle enum aliases point to new string values correctly
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 68 verified trade style renaming feature. All new style names (scalp, intraday, multi_day, swing, position) work correctly. Backwards compatibility aliases (move_2_move, trade_2_hold, a_plus) are preserved. API endpoints accept both old and new names. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_trade_style_renaming.py can be reused for regression testing.
